figures.caption: Figure 1: (Left) Comparison between the original FPS-based (CUDA-optimized) and FlashFPS-based PNN architecture. (Right) FlashFPS well maintains geometric features and achieves an average 5.16x inference speedup for PNNs.

Figure 6: FlashFPS: a plug-and-play acceleration framework that integrates FPS-Prune (in-layer pruning) and FPS-Cache (cross-layer reuse) to reduce redundancy and enable efficient FPS processing in PNNs.
Table 1: Evaluation of FPS and FlashFPS for semantic segmentation on S3DIS and ScanNet. FlashFPS-p integrates FPSPrune and FPS-Cache, where 𝑝 denotes the pruning ratio.
Figure 7: GPU inference speedup of FlashFPS on PointNeXtL and PointVector-L for segmenting S3DIS and ScanNet datasets, compared with FPS-CUDA (baseline) and QuickFPS.
